DRAFT 草稿 This entry is not yet published. It is hidden from the column index and from search engines. 本篇尚未发布——已从专栏索引与搜索引擎中隐藏。
Friday Harbor周五港
№ 007 Tuesday, April 28, 2026 2026年4月28日星期二 Earth · Orbit to Subterranean · 1904 ↔ 2026 地球 · 轨道到地下 · 1904 ↔ 2026

Mackinder, Redrawn 麦金德，重画

In 1904, Halford Mackinder gave the world a map of pivots and crescents. A century later, the world is a section drawing — six layers, six chokepoint sets, and a thesis with a hundred years of dust on it. 1904 年，麦金德给世界画了一张关于枢纽与新月的地图。一百二十年过去，世界已是一张剖面图——六个图层、六组咽喉点，加上一条积了一个世纪灰尘的论断。

In April 1904 Halford Mackinder stood up at the Royal Geographical Society and read out a paper that would haunt the next 122 years of foreign policy. The argument condensed to a syllogism: "Who rules East Europe commands the Heartland; who rules the Heartland commands the World-Island; who rules the World-Island commands the world." The Heartland was the inner mass of Eurasia — too cold for ships, newly-mobile because of railways, and beyond the reach of the sea-power empires that had governed the previous three centuries. The world Mackinder sketched was a flat one. It had a pivot, an inner crescent, and an outer crescent — three concentric rings on a Mercator surface.

The flatness is the part that has aged badly. In 2013 the political geographer Stuart Elden wrote a paper called Secure the Volume, pointing out that "secure the area" is now an inadequate verb because modern sovereignty is exercised through height and depth as much as through breadth. Drone strikes, subterranean mining, satellite surveillance, and submarine fibre cables are all governance acts that Mackinder's two-dimensional map cannot legibly contain. Elden's injunction was deceptively simple: think volume — through volume, with volume. The map needs a section.

So this column has tried to draw one. Six layers, top to bottom. Orbit: SpaceX has put 9,357 satellites in low-Earth orbit by December 2025; Eutelsat OneWeb has 650; Amazon Kuiper passed 200 in February. China is racing to lift two parallel mega-constellations (Guowang, SpaceSail) toward a combined 15,000 satellites, hampered mostly by a 7× cost gap on launch. In 2022 a single tweet from the CEO of the dominant constellation reportedly aborted a Ukrainian naval drone operation off Crimea. That is a verb the 1904 Heartland thesis cannot conjugate.

Atmosphere is thin in this taxonomy — drones and missiles mostly. Sea surface, by contrast, is thick. Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping cut Suez transits by fifty-eight per cent between November 2023 and October 2024, with each rerouted ship adding eleven thousand nautical miles, ten days, and a million dollars in fuel via the Cape. As one chokepoint failed, a second emerged: the Northern Sea Route along Russia's Arctic coast moved 37.9 million tonnes of cargo in 2024 — a record — and Russia and China have committed to lift that figure to twenty million tonnes of bilateral container freight by 2030. Mackinder lived to see the first ice-free Arctic summers theorised; he did not live to see them shipped through.

Sea floor is the layer Mackinder had no language for and the one most loaded today. Roughly ninety-five per cent of intercontinental data crosses a few hundred undersea fibre cables. Across November and December 2024, four cables were severed beneath the Baltic in three separate incidents — the Chinese-flagged Yi Peng 3 sailing out of a Russian port, then the Russian shadow-fleet tanker Eagle S dragging an anchor for sixty-two miles. American and European intelligence officials have, as of January 2025, downgraded these to "probably accidents." The downgrade is itself the point: hybrid warfare is precisely what cannot be conclusively distinguished from incompetence.

Land surface is where Mackinder's ghost still lives. Russia's war in Ukraine reads as straight Heartland strategy — buffer zones, access corridors, a continental power trying to push back the coastline of the rim. But the land surface holds another set of pivots Mackinder did not reckon with: Taiwan (where TSMC fabricates more than ninety per cent of the world's leading-edge logic chips), Northern Virginia's Loudoun County (where roughly seventy per cent of global internet traffic transits), Veldhoven in the Netherlands (where ASML alone makes the EUV lithography systems that everyone else's fabs depend on). The new pivots are smaller, networked, and frequently a single corporate campus.

Subterranean is the layer Mackinder could not have predicted and that may matter most by 2035. Refined gallium production: ninety- eight per cent China. Graphite: seventy-seven. Rare earths: sixty- nine, with heavy rare earths even higher. Cobalt: seventy-four per cent of mined supply from the Democratic Republic of the Congo. Nickel: half from Indonesia. Across copper, lithium, nickel, cobalt, graphite, and rare earths, the top-three refining nations together took eighty- six per cent of the market in 2024, up from eighty-two in 2020. In December 2024 China banned exports of gallium, germanium, and antimony to the United States; in early 2025 the list expanded to tungsten, tellurium, bismuth, indium, molybdenum, and seven heavy rare earths.

Read all six layers together and a different thesis emerges. Henry Farrell and Abraham Newman, in Underground Empire (2023), argue that the United States built its post-Cold-War power on weaponized interdependence — the structural advantage that comes from sitting at the centre of a few critical networks (submarine cables, dollar clearing, chip design, cloud compute) and from being able, when it chooses, to deny others access. The Chinese counter is a mirror image: not centred control of one network but cumulative control of the inputs everyone else's networks depend on. Refined silicon. Refined cobalt. Refined gallium. Refined gallium again, until the export ban is lifted.

The flatness Mackinder bequeathed has not gone away from policy language. The phrase "Heartland" is being used in 2025 by analysts arguing that Russia's war in Ukraine is a textbook geopolitical contest. They are not exactly wrong — they are reading one layer of a stack. The geopolitical reality of 2026 is not a flat board with a pivot in the middle. It is six interlocking layers, each with a handful of choke points, none of which can be controlled from any single capital. Whoever holds the most chokepoints across the most layers commands the most. Nobody commands the world.

What this section refuses to do. It does not draw the smuggling and grey-export channels that connect the layers — chip transhipment through Singapore and Malaysia, mineral laundering through Rwanda, dollar workarounds through stablecoins. Those are real, growing, and by design unmeasurable. Their absence here is cartographic honesty, not optimism. A future entry will try to draw them.

1904 年 4 月，Halford Mackinder 在英国皇家地理学会念了一篇文章， 此后 122 年里，外交政策的话语都被它笼罩。论证可以浓缩成一段 三段论：「谁控制东欧，谁就控制 Heartland（心脏地带）；谁控制 Heartland，谁就控制世界岛（欧亚大陆）；谁控制世界岛，谁就控制 世界。」Heartland 是欧亚大陆的内陆——太冷不能行船，又因为铁路 时代变得机动起来，从此可以与之前三个世纪统治世界的海权帝国 抗衡。麦金德画的世界是平的，有一个枢纽、一个内新月、一个外新月 ——三个同心圆，落在墨卡托投影上。

平面那部分老化得最厉害。2013 年，政治地理学家 Stuart Elden 写了 一篇文章叫 Secure the Volume，指出「占领面」（secure the area）已不足以描述今天主权的行使方式：现代主权同时在高度和深度上 运作。无人机打击、地下采矿、卫星侦察、海底光缆——这些治理行为 都在麦金德的二维地图里无处可装。Elden 的命题朴素得让人意外： 从体的角度思考——穿过体、与体一起思考。地图需要一个 剖面。

所以这一篇试着画一个剖面。六层，从上到下读。 轨道：到 2025 年 12 月，SpaceX 已把 9,357 颗 Starlink 送上低轨；Eutelsat OneWeb 有 650 颗；Amazon Kuiper 在 2025 年 2 月 过 200。中国正在猛追，「国网」与「千帆」两条平行巨型星座目标 合计 15,000 颗，主要被 7 倍的发射成本差距拖住。2022 年，主导 星座那家公司的 CEO 一条推文，据报让乌克兰在克里米亚外海的无人艇 行动流产。这是 1904 年的 Heartland 论断无法变位的一个动词。

大气这一层在我的分类里很薄——主要是无人机和导弹。 海面则厚得多。Houthi 在红海的袭击让苏伊士通过量在 2023 年 11 月到 2024 年 10 月之间下跌了 58%；每艘绕道的船经好望角 要多走 11,000 海里、10 天、一百万美元燃油。一个咽喉点失能时， 第二个被推到台前：俄罗斯北极沿海的 NSR（北方海航线）在 2024 年 货运 3,790 万吨——破纪录——而中俄已经定下 2030 年双边 集装箱货运 2,000 万吨的目标。麦金德在世时刚好赶上无冰夏季的 理论提出；他没活到看见货物从那里运过去的那一天。

海底是麦金德完全没有语言可以描述的一层，也是今天最敏感 的一层。约 95% 的跨洲数据走海底几百根光缆。2024 年 11 月与 12 月，波罗的海海底有 4 条电缆在三起独立事件中被切断——先是 从俄罗斯港口启航的中国旗船 Yi Peng 3，再是俄罗斯 "影子舰队"油轮 Eagle S 拖锚 62 英里。美欧情报机构 到 2025 年 1 月已把这些事件降级为「可能是事故」。这个降级本身 就说明问题：hybrid warfare（混合战争）的本质，恰恰是它无法被 明确地与"无能"区分开来。

地表这一层麦金德的鬼魂仍在。俄乌战争从 Heartland 战略的 视角读毫无违和感——缓冲区、通行廊道、大陆强权试图把边缘的海岸线 推回去。但地表层还有另一组麦金德没料到的枢纽：台湾（TSMC 制造 全球 90%+ 的最先进逻辑芯片）、北弗吉尼亚 Loudoun 县（约 70% 的 全球互联网流量经此中转）、荷兰 Veldhoven（ASML 一家公司生产 所有人的晶圆厂都依赖的 EUV 光刻机）。新的枢纽更小、更网络化， 经常就是一家公司的园区。

地下是麦金德无法预测的一层，也可能是 2035 年最关键的 一层。镓的精炼产量：98% 中国。石墨：77%。稀土：69%，重稀土更高。 钴：74% 来自刚果民主共和国的开采量。镍：印尼一家占一半。整个 铜、锂、镍、钴、石墨、稀土的精炼，前三国合计在 2024 年拿了 86%， 比 2020 年的 82% 还要更集中。2024 年 12 月，中国禁止镓、锗、锑 对美出口；2025 年初清单扩大到钨、碲、铋、铟、钼，加 7 种重稀土。

把六层一起读，一个不同的论断出现了。Henry Farrell 与 Abraham Newman 在 2023 年的 Underground Empire 里论证：美国 冷战后的霸权建在 weaponized interdependence 之上—— 它的结构性优势是坐在几个关键网络（海底光缆、美元清算、芯片 设计、云计算）的中心，可以选择性地切断别人的接入。中国的反制 是一面镜像：不是控制某一个网络的中心，而是累积控制别人所有 网络都需要的输入物。精炼硅。精炼钴。精炼镓。再一次精炼镓—— 直到出口禁令解除。

麦金德留下的"平"并没有从政策语言里退场。「Heartland」这个词 仍然被 2025 年的分析人士用来论证俄乌战争是教科书式的地缘政治 较量。他们没说错——他们读的只是这个堆栈里的一层。2026 年的 地缘政治现实不是一张中间有枢纽的平面棋盘。它是六个互锁的 图层，每层都有几个咽喉点，没有任何一个能从任何单一首都被控制。 谁能在更多层叠加更多咽喉点谁控制更多。没有人控制世界。

这张剖面图刻意没做的事。没有画连接各层的走私与灰色出口 通道——经新加坡马来西亚的芯片转口、经卢旺达的矿产洗白、绕开 美元的稳定币结算。这些都是真实的、增长中的、按设计就不可计量的。 它们在这里的缺席是制图诚实，不是乐观。下一篇会试着画它们。

Endnote尾注

  • Mackinder's original argument: Halford J. Mackinder, "The Geographical Pivot of History," The Geographical Journal 23(4), 1904; expanded in Democratic Ideals and Reality (1919). The 1904 paper is in the public domain.
  • Vertical / volumetric framing: Stuart Elden, "Secure the Volume: Vertical Geopolitics and the Depth of Power," Political Geography, 2013; further developed in Voluminous States (Duke, 2020).
  • Network / chokepoint framing: Henry Farrell & Abraham Newman, Underground Empire: How America Weaponized the World Economy (Henry Holt, 2023); their earlier "weaponized interdependence" article in International Security 44(1), 2019.
  • Heartland thesis revival in 2024–2025 commentary: Geopolitical Monitor, Eurasia Review, RealClearWorld.
  • Orbital layer: Starlink (9,357 satellites, Dec 2025); MERICS, "Orbital geopolitics"; FPRI, "LEO Wars" (Apr 2025); Foreign Policy, "Starlink Has Privatized Geopolitics" (Mar 2026).
  • Sea-floor layer: 2024 Baltic Sea submarine cable disruptions; Lieber Institute West Point; Washington Post, "Accidents, not sabotage"; Atlantic Council.
  • Sea-surface layer: Atlas Institute, Red Sea crisis 2024–25; DocShipper; Centre for High North Logistics, NSR 2025; gCaptain (NSR 37.9 Mt 2024); TASS — 20 Mt by 2030 Russia-China target.
  • Subterranean layer: IEA, Global Critical Minerals Outlook 2025; Rare Earth Exchanges.
  • Land-surface layer: Tom's Hardware, ASML EUV concentration; Chris Miller, Chip War (2022). Loudoun figures cross-referenced from the earlier Where the Cloud Touches Ground entry in this column.
  • 麦金德原文：Halford J. Mackinder, "The Geographical Pivot of History"，The Geographical Journal 23(4)，1904；后续展开见 Democratic Ideals and Reality（1919）。1904 年原文已进入公共领域。
  • 垂直 / 体积地缘理论：Stuart Elden, "Secure the Volume"，Political Geography，2013；后续展开见 Voluminous States（Duke, 2020）。
  • 网络 / 咽喉点框架：Henry Farrell & Abraham Newman，Underground Empire: How America Weaponized the World Economy（Henry Holt, 2023）；早期论文 "weaponized interdependence"，International Security 44(1)，2019。
  • 2024–2025 关于 Heartland 论复兴的评论：Geopolitical Monitor、Eurasia Review、RealClearWorld。
  • 轨道层：Starlink（2025-12 共 9,357 颗）；MERICS《Orbital geopolitics》；FPRI《LEO Wars》（2025-04）；Foreign Policy《Starlink Has Privatized Geopolitics》（2026-03）。
  • 海底层：2024 年波罗的海海底光缆事件；Lieber Institute West Point；Washington Post：「事故而非破坏」；Atlantic Council。
  • 海面层：Atlas Institute《2024–25 红海航运危机》；DocShipper；Centre for High North Logistics（NSR 2025）；gCaptain（NSR 2024 年 3,790 万吨）；TASS——中俄目标 2030 年 2,000 万吨。
  • 地下层：IEA《Global Critical Minerals Outlook 2025》；Rare Earth Exchanges。
  • 地表层：Tom's Hardware：ASML EUV 集中度；Chris Miller，Chip War（2022）。Loudoun 数据交叉参考本专栏前作《Where the Cloud Touches Ground》。
